title: Swearing a False Oath (Al-Yamin Al-Ghamus)
subtitle: Invoking Allah's Name to Confirm a Lie — A Sin That Plunges Into Hell

verses.arabic: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يَشْتَرُونَ بِعَهْدِ اللَّهِ وَأَيْمَانِهِمْ ثَمَنًا قَلِيلًا أُولَٰئِكَ لَا خَلَاقَ لَهُمْ فِي الْآخِرَةِ وَلَا يُكَلِّمُهُمُ اللَّهُ وَلَا يَنظُرُ إِلَيْهِمْ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ وَلَا يُزَكِّيهِمْ وَلَهُمْ عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌ
verses.translation: Indeed, those who exchange the covenant of Allah and their [own] oaths for a small price will have no share in the Hereafter, and Allah will not speak to them or look at them on the Day of Resurrection, nor will He purify them; and they will have a painful punishment.

verses.arabic: وَلَا تَتَّخِذُوا أَيْمَانَكُمْ دَخَلًا بَيْنَكُمْ فَتَزِلَّ قَدَمٌ بَعْدَ ثُبُوتِهَا وَتَذُوقُوا السُّوءَ بِمَا صَدَدتُّمْ عَن سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ
verses.translation: And do not take your oaths as [means of] deceit between you, lest a foot slip after it was [once] firm, and you would taste evil [in consequence] of what you hindered from the way of Allah.

verses.arabic: وَلَا تَشْتَرُوا بِعَهْدِ اللَّهِ ثَمَنًا قَلِيلًا ۚ إِنَّ مَا عِندَ اللَّهِ هُوَ خَيْرٌ لَّكُمْ
verses.translation: And do not exchange the covenant of Allah for a small price. Indeed, what is with Allah is best for you, if only you could know.

brief_explanation: Al-Yamin al-Ghamus is the most severe category of false oath — swearing by Allah while knowingly lying in order to consume another's right or deceive others. The Quran describes those who sell Allah's covenant for a trivial price of worldly gain as facing three devastating consequences: Allah will not speak to them, will not look at them, and will not purify them on the Day of Resurrection — followed by a painful punishment. Invoking the name of Allah to authenticate a lie is not merely dishonesty; it is a desecration of the Most Sacred Name and an act of profound disrespect toward the One who knows all secrets. The word 'ghamus' (plunging) in Arabic refers to its nature: it plunges the swearer deep into sin.
capital_punishment: There is no hadd (fixed corporal punishment) prescribed for false oaths in general. The Yamin al-Ghamus requires sincere tawbah and, if it caused another person to lose their wealth or rights, full restitution of what was taken. No kaffarah (expiation) is accepted for this type of oath — the scholars are agreed that its gravity goes beyond what kaffarah can cover, making tawbah and restitution the only path to correction. The state may impose ta'zir for false oaths used in judicial proceedings, especially when they led to unjust judgments.
